results.resolved_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
results.note: Correct paper/organism (X. campestris pv. vesicatoria 85-10 = X. euvesicatoria, XCV tags). Abstract confirms Xps T2SS secretes 2 xylanases, a protease, a lipase, but does not name xynB2/XCV4358. Full text not open access (PMC not OA, publisher 403); gene-level mapping not in retrievable text.

results.resolved_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
results.note: Same as xynB2: correct paper/organism/system per abstract, but specific gene name xynB3/XCV4360 not in abstract and full text not retrievable (paywall).

results.resolved_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
results.note: Abstract names only 'two type II secreted predicted xylanases'; cannot confirm xynB1/XCV0965 individually. Full text paywalled.

results.resolved_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
results.note: Abstract confirms a T2S-secreted protease but does not name prtA/XCV3671; full text paywalled.

results.resolved_title: Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria Secretes Proteases and Xylanases via the Xps Type II Secretion System and Outer Membrane Vesicles
results.note: Abstract confirms a T2S-secreted lipase but does not name lipA/XCV0536; full text paywalled.

results.resolved_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
results.quote: The extracellular alkaline protease produced by Pseudomonas aeruginosa is secreted by a specific pathway, independent of the pathway used by most of the other extracellular proteins of this organism.
results.note: P. aeruginosa alkaline protease (apr/aprA) secreted by an ABC/HlyD/TolC-type pathway (T1SS); complemented by HlyB/HlyD/TolC and PrtDEF. Organism matches PAO1 entry.

results.resolved_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
results.note: Entry is for Pseudomonas chlororaphis O6 (PchlO6_3385). The cited paper is exclusively about P. aeruginosa alkaline protease; it makes no claim about P. chlororaphis. Homolog-from-different-species provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
results.note: Entry is for Pseudomonas fluorescens Pf-5 (PFL_3210). Cited paper is about P. aeruginosa only; no claim about P. fluorescens. Homolog-from-different-species provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: The secretion genes of Pseudomonas aeruginosa alkaline protease are functionally related to those of Erwinia chrysanthemi proteases and Escherichia coli alpha-haemolysin
results.note: Entry is for Pseudomonas fragi (NCTC10754_03170). Cited paper is about P. aeruginosa only; no claim about P. fragi. Homolog-from-different-species provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: New secreted toxins and immunity proteins encoded within the Type VI secretion system gene cluster of Serratia marcescens Db10
results.quote: Ssp1 and Ssp2 were shown to be secreted substrates of the T6SS by immunoblotting secreted fractions from the wild type strain, two T6SS mutants
results.note: Serratia marcescens Db10. Ssp1 (SMA2261 in paper) confirmed T6SS-secreted antibacterial toxin.

results.resolved_title: New secreted toxins and immunity proteins encoded within the Type VI secretion system gene cluster of Serratia marcescens Db10
results.quote: Ssp1 and Ssp2 were shown to be secreted substrates of the T6SS by immunoblotting secreted fractions from the wild type strain, two T6SS mutants
results.note: Serratia marcescens Db10. Ssp2 (SMA2264 in paper) confirmed T6SS-secreted antibacterial toxin.

results.resolved_title: New secreted toxins and immunity proteins encoded within the Type VI secretion system gene cluster of Serratia marcescens Db10
results.quote: Ssp1 and Ssp2 were shown to be secreted substrates of the T6SS by immunoblotting secreted fractions from the wild type strain, two T6SS mutants
results.note: Same paper; Ssp1 = SMA2261. Locus tag SMDB11_2261 corresponds to the paper's Ssp1; organism field blank but paper is S. marcescens Db10.

results.resolved_title: New secreted toxins and immunity proteins encoded within the Type VI secretion system gene cluster of Serratia marcescens Db10
results.quote: Ssp1 and Ssp2 were shown to be secreted substrates of the T6SS by immunoblotting secreted fractions from the wild type strain, two T6SS mutants
results.note: Same paper; Ssp2 = SMA2264. Locus tag SMDB11_2264 corresponds to the paper's Ssp2; organism field blank but paper is S. marcescens Db10.

results.resolved_title: Virulence Factors of Aeromonas hydrophila: In the Wake of Reclassification
results.note: Full review text retrieved. It is a review of Aeromonas virulence factors; the T2SS section names only Act as a T2SS substrate. GCAT/satA is not described as a T2SS substrate (not characterized here). No T2SS-secretion evidence for this gene in ATCC 7966.

results.resolved_title: Virulence Factors of Aeromonas hydrophila: In the Wake of Reclassification
results.note: Full review text retrieved. ahpA/ahpB (ahp) discussed only as extracellular protease/elastase of strain AG2 within pathotype strings, never tied to the T2SS or to ATCC 7966. No T2SS-secretion evidence for ahpA.

results.resolved_title: Virulence Factors of Aeromonas hydrophila: In the Wake of Reclassification
results.note: Full review text retrieved. ahpB mentioned only as a secreted elastase of strain AG2; not linked to T2SS or ATCC 7966. No T2SS-secretion evidence for ahpB.

results.resolved_title: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa T6SS-VgrG1b spike is topped by a PAAR protein eliciting DNA damage to bacterial competitors
results.note: Paper (P. aeruginosa T6SS) is entirely about Tse7/Tsi7. Tse5_RhsP1 (PA2684) is not in title or abstract; full text empty in PMC and not otherwise retrievable, so cannot confirm or counter. Absence from un-retrieved full text = INACCESSIBLE.

results.resolved_title: The Pseudomonas aeruginosa T6SS-VgrG1b spike is topped by a PAAR protein eliciting DNA damage to bacterial competitors
results.quote: We find that Tse7 sits on the tip of the VgrG1b spike and that specific residues at the PAAR-VgrG1b interface are essential for VgrG1b-dependent delivery of Tse7 into bacterial prey.
results.note: P. aeruginosa T6SS toxin Tse7 (PA0099), a Tox-GHH2 nuclease delivered via the T6SS. Strongly supported by abstract.

results.resolved_title: Initiation of RNA Polymerization and Polymerase Encapsidation by a Small dsRNA Virus
results.note: DOI resolves (PubMed + crossref) to a human picobirnavirus RdRP structure paper, not an Ehrlichia chaffeensis T4SS effector paper. No claim about Etf-2/ECH_1027. Wrong-DOI provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: Initiation of RNA Polymerization and Polymerase Encapsidation by a Small dsRNA Virus
results.note: Same DOI resolves to a picobirnavirus RdRP paper, not Ehrlichia chaffeensis. No claim about Etf-3/ECH_1136. Wrong-DOI provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: Identification and distribution of new candidate T6SS effectors encoded in Salmonella Pathogenicity Island 6
results.note: Full text retrieved. Correct organism/system (Salmonella SPI-6 T6SS, includes Typhimurium 14028s). Paper names the ART effector Tre = STM0291 (Jurenas 2022) and surveys Rhs effectors, but does not name Tre1_RhsA / locus STM14_0271 specifically. Cannot quote support for this exact gene.

results.resolved_title: Identification and distribution of new candidate T6SS effectors encoded in Salmonella Pathogenicity Island 6
results.note: Full text retrieved. Survey of SPI-6 T6SS Rhs effectors in Salmonella; does not name Tre2_RhsB / locus STM14_0272 specifically. Cannot quote support for this exact gene.

results.resolved_title: Essential Role of the Type III Secretion System Effector NleB in Colonization of Mice by Citrobacter rodentium
results.quote: we showed that both NleB and NleE were translocated into host cells by the LEE-encoded T3SS of enteropathogenic
results.note: Citrobacter rodentium (mouse A/E pathogen). NleB is a LEE-encoded T3SS effector translocated into host cells; deletion reduces colonization.

results.resolved_title: Evidence that CT694 is a novel Chlamydia trachomatis T3S substrate capable of functioning during invasion or early cycle development
results.quote: These data indicate that CT694 is a novel T3S-dependent substrate unique to C. trachomatis, and that its interaction with host proteins such as AHNAK may be important for aspects of invasion or development particular to this species.
results.note: Chlamydia trachomatis CT694 (TmeA) is a T3SS substrate. (Note: the PMC full-text API mis-served an unrelated S. aureus PBP1 article for this PMCID; PubMed metadata + abstract are the correct CT694 paper.)

results.resolved_title: A phospholipase A1 antibacterial Type VI secretion effector interacts directly with the C-terminal domain of the VgrG spike protein for delivery
results.quote: We further identify Tle1, a toxin effector encoded by this cluster and showed that Tle1 possesses phospholipase A1 and A2 activities required for the interbacterial competition.
results.note: Enteroaggregative E. coli (Sci-1 T6SS). Tle1 is a T6SS phospholipase effector delivered via the VgrG spike; consistent with E. coli 042 / EC042_4528.

results.resolved_title: Assembly of the type II secretion system: identification of ExeA residues critical for peptidoglycan binding and secretin multimerization
results.quote: Aeromonas hydrophila secretes a number of protein toxins across the outer membrane via the type II secretion system (T2SS).
results.note: Aeromonas hydrophila. Paper uses lipase secretion as the T2SS readout ('Five of the substitution derivatives showed greatly decreased lipase secretion'), confirming lipase (lip) is a T2SS substrate.

results.resolved_title: The Plasmodium rhoptry associated protein complex is important for parasitophorous vacuole membrane structure and intraerythrocytic parasite growth
results.note: DOI resolves (crossref) to a Plasmodium rhoptry-associated-protein paper, not an Ehrlichia chaffeensis T4SS effector paper. No claim about NlmA/ECH_0822. Wrong-DOI provenance defect.

results.resolved_title: Legionella pneumophila Modulates Mitochondrial Dynamics to Trigger Metabolic Repurposing of Infected Macrophages
results.quote: The T4SS effector MitF, a Ran GTPase activator, is required for fission of the mitochondrial network.
results.note: Legionella pneumophila. MitF (lpg2818) is an injected T4SS effector. Matches claim.

results.resolved_title: Principles for designing ideal protein structures
results.note: DOI resolves (PubMed + crossref) to a Baker-lab computational protein-design paper, not a T6SS effector paper. No claim about Tle1/PA3290 or any secretion system. Wrong-DOI provenance defect (the real Tle1 paper is Russell et al. Nature 2013, 10.1038/nature12074).

results.resolved_title: Type VI Secretion System Transports Zn2+ to Combat Multiple Stresses and Host Immunity
results.quote: These results establish that YezP is a substrate secreted by T6SS-4.
results.note: Yersinia pseudotuberculosis YPIII. The paper's T6SS-4 secreted substrate is YezP = Ypk_3549; YPK_RS17835 is the modern RefSeq tag for the same gene. Organism field blank (no conflict).

results.resolved_title: Characterization of Pic, a Secreted Protease of Shigella flexneri and Enteroaggregative Escherichia coli
results.quote: We have identified and characterized a secreted protein, designated Pic, which is encoded on the chromosomes of enteroaggregative (EAEC) 042 and 2457T.
results.note: Pic is a secreted SPATE autotransporter (Type V secretion) of EAEC 042, processed during secretion and released into culture supernatant. Matches T5SS / E. coli 042 claim.
